title: primula russeola subsp. lancifolia
intro: Primula russeola subsp. lancifolia, a lesser-known subspecies of the Russian Primrose, is a flowering plant that belongs to the Primulaceae family. Its leaves and roots are used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues, due to its expectorant and antiseptic properties. In herbal preparations, the roots and leaves of Primula russeola subsp. lancifolia can be dried and made into teas, infusions, or infuse it in oil for medicinal use.
